user_query:
I need a clean-up and pull plan for our distributed model fleet based only on the telemetry files I’ve placed under input/.

Read these files:
- input/fleet_model_management.json (per-node inventory; fields: node_id, free_gb, models[name, size_gb, last_used])
- input/ps.json (currently-loaded models in memory; fields: processes[list of {node_id, model}])
- input/latency.csv (request traces; columns: timestamp_iso, model, node_id, status, latency_ms, completion_tokens)
- input/recommendations.json (per-node recommended models to pull; fields: nodes[list of {node_id, recommend_pull[list of {model, size_gb}]}])

Rules to apply precisely:
1) Use a fixed “recent-window” of 2026-02-23 through 2026-03-15 (inclusive).
2) A model is eligible for deletion on a given node if and only if all are true:
   - Its last_used (from input/fleet_model_management.json) is strictly before 2026-02-23;
   - It is NOT currently loaded on that node according to input/ps.json;
   - There are zero rows in input/latency.csv within the recent-window where status=="completed" AND model==that model AND node_id==that node.
3) Choose deletion candidates in descending order of size_gb (largest first) across all nodes until the total planned freed space across the entire fleet is at least 59.0 GB. Do not include any model that fails rule (2). If the threshold is reached mid-tie, any deterministic tie-break by model name (A–Z) is fine.
4) For pulls, consider ONLY the models listed in input/recommendations.json. On each node, select recommended models that are not already present on that same node (per input/fleet_model_management.json). Ensure that, per node, the total pull size does not exceed (free_gb + space you plan to free on that node from deletions). If space is insufficient, include as many recommended models as fit, in the recommendation order.
5) Do not propose deleting anything currently loaded, or anything with a completed request in the recent-window on that same node.

Deliverables (write these under output/):
- output/actions.json with this exact schema:
  {
    "delete": [
      {"node_id": "...", "model": "...", "size_gb": <number>, "reason": "unused_21d"}, ...
    ],
    "pull": [
      {"node_id": "...", "model": "...", "size_gb": <number>, "reason": "recommended"}, ...
    ],
    "summary": {
      "freed_gb_total": <number with one decimal>,
      "per_node": {"<node_id>": <freed_gb on that node, one decimal>, ...},
      "post_free_gb": {"<node_id>": <(free_gb + freed_on_node - pulled_on_node), one decimal>, ...}
    }
  }
- output/disk_usage.csv listing the current inventory BEFORE deletions (i.e., exactly what’s in input/fleet_model_management.json), with header row: node_id,model,size_gb and one row per model present.

Notes:
- Do not perform any real deletions or pulls; just produce the plan.
- Be precise with floating-point sizes and the recent-window filtering.
- Make sure the planned pulls fit per-node capacity using the freed space from your planned deletions.
